QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_20

question.text: What is the main purpose of a New Fund Offer (NFO)?

answer.text: To launch a new mutual fund scheme and collect initial investment from investors
To offer existing scheme units at a discounted price
To merge two existing mutual fund schemes
To return money to existing investors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An NFO (New Fund Offer) is when an AMC launches a brand new mutual fund scheme for the first time. During the NFO period (usually 15-30 days), investors can subscribe to the scheme at the initial offer price (usually Rs. 10 per unit). After the NFO closes, the fund starts investing and trading begins. **The Trap:** An NFO is NOT a discount sale of existing schemes. It is the launch of a completely new scheme. Also, NFO units at Rs. 10 are not "cheaper" — they simply have not yet generated any returns. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under fund launch process. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Do not push NFOs just because the Rs. 10 NAV looks "cheap." A fund with NAV of Rs. 100 is not expensive — it just has a track record. For retired clients, established funds with proven track records are often better than new, untested NFOs.
